title: Right to Education Denial
description: In June 2024, Sita Devi applied for admission of her 8-year-old son, Mohan, to the nearest government primary school in her village. The school denied admission citing lack of available seats and absence of required documents. Despite repeated requests, the child was not admitted. An RTI filed by a local NGO revealed vacant seats and non-compliance with RTE norms, leading to a legal challenge for violation of the right to free and compulsory education.

characters.name: Sita Devi
characters.details: Mother of Mohan, daily wage worker seeking school admission for her son

characters.name: Government Primary School, Rampur
characters.details: Local government school that denied admission

characters.name: Rakesh Yadav
characters.details: School authority responsible for admission decisions

characters.name: Seema Khan
characters.details: Filed RTI and uncovered discrepancies in school records

evidence: Government circular on RTE admission norms
Application form submitted by Sita Devi
Affidavit from parent regarding repeated attempts
NGO report on RTE violations in the area
evidence_chain: Child applied for admission to nearest school
School denied admission citing lack of seats
RTI reveals availability of vacant seats
Failure to provide education violates Article 21A
Authorities failed to justify denial lawfully
legal_ambiguity: Whether administrative reasons like documentation or claimed lack of seats can justify denial despite statutory mandate of free and compulsory education.
